title: Ayesha
content:
{{CharHub|Ayesha|Ayesha}} {{Character |image = Ayesha profile (1).png |real name = Ayesha{{Ref|GotGV2}} |species = [[Sovereign]] |gender = Female |DOD = [[2026|Mid-Late 2026]]{{Ref|GotGV3}}[[Disney+|Disney+ - "Marvel Cinematic Universe in Timeline Order"]] |title = Golden High Priestess |movie = ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2]]''
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3]]'' |book = ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2: Meet the Team!]]''
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2: The Return of Rocket and Groot]]'' |actor = [[Elizabeth Debicki]] |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|[[Guardians of the Galaxy|Guardians]], perhaps it will provide you solace that your deaths are not without purpose. They will serve as a warning to all of those tempted with betraying us. Don't screw with the [[Sovereign]].|Ayesha|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} '''Ayesha''' was the Golden High Priestess of the [[Sovereign]], a race created by the [[High Evolutionary]]. She hired the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] to protect [[Anulax Batteries]] from an [[Abilisk]], but after [[Rocket]] stole several of the batteries with the intention to later sell them, she became enraged and sent her fleet of [[Omnicrafts]] to kill them. The Guardians managed to escape through a [[Quantum Asteroid Field]], and Ayesha later hired [[Yondu Udonta]] and his [[Ravagers]] to complete the task, only to later be betrayed by him. After gaining information from [[Taserface]], Ayesha's army was unsuccessful in another attempt to destroy the Guardians. Ayesha then created the Sovereign's greatest weapon, [[Adam Warlock]], to finally get revenge on the Guardians. She and Warlock worked with the High Evolutionary to capture [[Rocket]], before Ayesha was caught in an explosion on [[Counter-Earth]] and killed by her creator. ==Biography== ===Leader of the Sovereign=== ====High Evolutionary's Creation==== {{Quote|I created [[Sovereign|you lot]] as merely an aesthetic experiment. Beautiful numbskulls.|[[High Evolutionary]] to Ayesha|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3}} The [[High Evolutionary]] created a race of gold humanoid beings, known as the [[Sovereign]], who considered themselves at a higher plane than everyone else. One of the Sovereign, Ayesha, was placed in charge, being given the title of Golden High Priestess, ruling over their [[Sovereign (Planet)|planet]]. As ruler of Sovereign, Ayesha was in charge of the creation of its citizens, designing them as specifically designed by the community in [[Birth Pods]] to be perfect.{{Ref|GotGV3}} ====Deal with the Guardians==== [[File:GotGV2 HD Stills 1.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha greets the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]]]] {{Quote|Well, perhaps someday, you could give me a history lesson in the archaic ways of our ancestors, for academic purposes.|Ayesha to [[Star-Lord]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} Due to the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]]'s new reputation as heroes, Ayesha, hired the Guardians to defend valuable [[Anulax Batteries]] from an inter-dimensional monster, the [[Abilisk]], in exchange for [[Nebula]], whom the [[Sovereign]] captured after she tried to steal the batteries. [[File:GotGV2 - Big Game 4.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha asks [[Peter Quill]] about his heritage]] Once the Guardians had completed the job, they headed to the [[Sovereign Throne Room]], where Ayesha thanked them, claiming that they could not risk the lives of her own people. Ayesha educated the Guardians on how the Sovereign were created, which [[Peter Quill]] remarked about preferring the natural way of procreation. Ayesha was intrigued by Quill and even suggested the two of them partaking in procreating. [[Gamora]] returned to their arrangement, so Ayesha had Nebula brought out and told Gamora to do with her as she pleased. Before they left, Ayesha asked Quill about his heritage, sensing that he was a hybrid of unorthodox genealogy.{{Ref|GotGV2}} ====Pursuing the Guardians==== [[File:Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 198.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha orders the [[Guardians of the Galaxy|Guardians]] be destroyed]] {{Quote|Our concern is their slight against our people. We hired [[Guardians of the Galaxy|them]] and they steal from us. It is heresy of the highest order.|Ayesha to [[Sovereign Admiral|Admiral]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} Although the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] accomplished their mission, [[Rocket]] stole several of the [[Anulax Batteries]] for himself. When the [[Sovereign]] discovered this, they attacked the ''[[Milano]]'', with their own fleet of [[Omnicrafts|Sovereign drones]]. The ''Milano'' was heavily damaged and forced to crash-land on a nearby planet, but the rest of the [[Omnicrafts|Sovereign fleet]] were single-handedly destroyed by [[Ego]]. [[File:GotGV2 Villains 8.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha makes a deal with [[Yondu Udonta]]]] Still desiring to get her revenge against the Guardians, Ayesha went to [[Contraxia]] to hire [[Yondu Udonta]] and his [[Yondu Ravager Clan|Ravagers]] to capture the Guardians. Arriving on Contraxia, Ayesha had her personal aides roll out a blue carpet in front of her so that she would not have to walk on the planet's snowy ground. Ayesha greeted Udonta and offered him one million [[Units]] to successfully capture and bring the Guardians to her so she can kill them. Udonta went against his deal with Ayesha, but [[Taserface]] supplied her with the location of Udonta and the Guardians.{{Ref|GotGV2}} ====[[Battle on Ego's Planet]]==== [[File:GotGV2 Villains 3.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha attacks the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]]]] {{Quote|Hey, remember that Ayesha chick?"
"Yeah, why? Oh, hell!|[[Kraglin Obfonteri]] and [[Yondu Udonta]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} Ayesha later led a [[Sovereign]] fleet against the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] as they were [[Battle on Ego's Planet|attacking the core]] of [[Ego]]. Ayesha piloted one of the [[Omnicrafts]] and declared to the Guardians that their deaths would serve as a warning to others not to go against the Sovereign. The whole fleet was eventually destroyed, much to Ayesha's anger and dismay.{{Ref|GotGV2}} ====Creating Adam==== [[File:GotGV2 Adam 2.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha admires her creation of [[Adam Warlock|Adam]]]] {{Quote|That, my child, is the next step in [[Sovereign|our]] evolution. More powerful. More beautiful. More capable of destroying the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]]. I think I shall call him... Adam.|Ayesha to her [[Sovereign Chambermaid]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} Ayesha's obsession to kill the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] festered, so she decided to develop a new breed of [[Sovereign]] powerful enough to destroy the Guardians. One of Ayesha's [[Sovereign Chambermaid|chambermaids]] told her that the council were waiting to speak with her, feeling that she had wasted their resources on her creation. However, Ayesha was certain that it was the next step in the Sovereign evolution, whom she would name [[Adam Warlock|Adam]].{{Ref|GotGV2}} ====Releasing Adam==== {{Quote|You removed [[Adam Warlock|Adam]] from his cocoon early, my liege. He is still a child.|Ayesha to [[High Evolutionary]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3}} By [[2026]], [[Adam Warlock|Adam]] was still gestating in his [[Birth Pods|Birth Pod]] when [[High Evolutionary]] demanded the premature release of the subject, being known as the Warlock. High Evolutionary planned to use Adam to capture a former subject, [[89P13]], or as Ayesha knew him, [[Rocket]]. Ayesha warned High Evolutionary about releasing her son too early, but High Evolutionary did not care.{{Ref|GotGV3}} ====Reporting to High Evolutionary==== [[File:Adam Warlock with his mommy.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha tending to her [[Adam Warlock|son]]'s wound]] {{Quote|You knew of [[89P13]]'s existence for years without alerting me. That's not what I require!"
"I'm sorry, my liege."
"Find 89P13 and return it to me. Or, I will destroy your entire civilization as is my right as your maker!|[[High Evolutionary]] and Ayesha|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3}} [[Adam Warlock|Adam]] returned from his [[Attack on Rocket|mission]] with a stab wound through his chest. A devastated Ayesha quickly grabbed a [[Med-Paks|Med-Pak]] and placed it on his injury. [[High Evolutionary]] arrived to see the failure of the Warlock. Ayesha stood up for her son, saying that the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] were more powerful than them. [[File:Ayesha inspected.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha is examined by [[High Evolutionary]]]] However, High Evolutionary, standing over her, responded that Ayesha overestimated her own value to him, detailing how the [[Sovereign]] were built aesthetically superior, but intellectually inferior. High Evolutionary inspected Ayesha, grabbing her head, forcing her eyes open, and telling her to open her mouth. Ayesha noted that Adam was removed from his cocoon too early, but High Evolutionary felt there was something wrong with him. And when Adam spoke against High Evolutionary, he used his gravity powers to throw Adam against a wall. [[File:Ayesha Vol 3.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha is threatened by [[High Evolutionary]]]] Ayesha cowered before High Evolutionary and apologized for her son not knowing any better, promising to do whatever he wanted from them. High Evolutionary turned his anger towards Ayesha for knowing about [[Rocket]]'s existence for years without reporting it to him. While hurting Adam, High Evolutionary then gave Ayesha an ultimatum, bring him Rocket or the Sovereign would be killed. When High Evolutionary asked if she understood, Ayesha said that she did while he was degradingly puppeteering her mouth.{{Ref|GotGV3}} ====Interrogating Fitz-Gibbonok==== [[File:Ayesha interrogates.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha interrogating [[Fitz-Gibbonok]]]] {{Quote|[[Fitz-Gibbonok]], it's me. Outpost, come in."
"Yes, [[Gamora/Cosmic Time Heist|Gamora]]. Salutations. May I help?"
"Well, I need you guys to come pick me up. I'm gonna upload the location link to the [[Bowie|craft]] I'm on."
"Oh, I think we can arrange that.|[[Gamora/Cosmic Time Heist|Gamora]] and Ayesha|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3}} When it was reported that the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] [[Infiltration into the Orgoscope|infiltrated]] the [[Orgoscope]], Ayesha and [[Adam Warlock|Adam]] went to the biological planet. Upon arrival, Ayesha picked up a [[United Ravagers|Ravager]] outside of the Orgoscope for interrogation. She sat [[Fitz-Gibbonok]] down with his pet, [[Blurp]]. They then tried to interrogate Fitz-Gibbonok, who would not talk. Ayesha told Adam to show him they meant business. [[File:Ayesha talks to Gamora.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha learns the [[Guardians of the Galaxy|Guardians]]'s destination]] Not knowing what she meant, Adam wrongly obliterated Fitz-Gibbonok, much to Ayesha's anger. This caused [[Kwol]] to chuckle as Ayesha berated Adam for his insolence. However, Adam was only focused on Blurp, who was very sad about his owner. Adam decided to take Blurp under his wing. Suddenly, Fitz-Gibbonok's transmitter beeped. Ayesha picked it up to find [[Gamora/Cosmic Time Heist|Gamora]] on the other side. Gamora thought Ayesha was a Ravager and gave her the location of the ''[[Bowie]]'', much to Ayesha's delight.{{Ref|GotGV3}} ====Arriving at Counter-Earth==== [[File:Adam Warlock with Ayesha.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha talks to her [[Adam Warlock|son]] about their stakes]] {{Quote|[[Gamora/Cosmic Time Heist|Gamora]] is [[Counter-Earth|here]]. Which means the [[Rocket Raccoon|squirrel]] is also. To save ourselves, we must bring it to the [[High Evolutionary]] before his own people do.|Ayesha to [[Adam Warlock|Adam]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3}} On the way to [[Counter-Earth]], which was the location of [[Gamora/Cosmic Time Heist|Gamora]], [[Adam Warlock|Adam]] brought aboard [[Blurp]]. Ayesha and Adam argued about this as Ayesha said he was just an animal and had no business being with them. She then ordered Adam to kill it. However, he refused to give Blurp up, wishing to train it in the ways of the Sovereign. [[File:Ayesha Before Death.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ayesha dies in [[Counter-Earth]]'s destruction]] When they got to Counter-Earth, Ayesha reiterated to her son that it was important they were the ones who captured [[Rocket]] before any of [[High Evolutionary]]'s forces. Once they landed, Adam took off towards the ''[[Bowie]]'' to capture Rocket while Ayesha stayed on the ship. A little later, Ayesha heard commotion so she looked out of her ship to see Counter-Earth being destroyed. She then saw Adam racing towards her. Despite Adam's attempts to save her, the ship was blown up and Ayesha was killed.{{Ref|GotGV3}} ==Abilities== {{Quote|Every citizen is born exactly as designed by the community. Impeccable, both physically and mentally. We control the DNA of our progeny, germinating them in birthing pods.|Ayesha|Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Ayesha possessed a high level of intelligence, something that she took great pride in, and often made her feel superior when comparing herself to other species, even towards other Sovereign citizens. She alone was able to genetically re-engineer her race into [[Adam Warlock|something]] more powerful. *'''Master Tactician''': As the leader of the [[Sovereign]], Ayesha was responsible for many decisions including, hiring the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]], attacking them for treason, and creating [[Adam Warlock]]. *'''Expert Pilot''': Ayesha was capable of piloting an aircraft that is remotely controlled by the pilot in the planet. The system then feeds into the actual aircraft that is in battle. ==Equipment== ===Vehicles=== *'''[[Omnicrafts]]''': As [[Priestesses of the Sovereign|High Priestess]] of [[Sovereign]], Ayesha commanded an army of Omnicrafts. She deployed this army against the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] in order to seek retribution for their theft of [[Anulax Batteries]]. *'''[[Ayesha's Ship]]''': Ayesha often used her own aircraft. She used this on her mission to find [[Rocket]]. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Med-Paks|Med-Pak]]''': Ayesha used a med-pak on [[Adam Warlock]] to heal his wound in the [[Arête Laboratories]]. ==Facilities== *'''[[Sovereign Throne Room]]''': Ayesha ruled [[Sovereign (Planet)|Sovereign]] from the throne room. She thanked the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] for fighting the [[Abilisk]] for them in the throne room. She offered them [[Nebula]] as a reward while in the throne room. *'''[[Sovereign Mission Control Centre]]''': Ayesha commanded an army of [[Omnicrafts]] from the mission control center on [[Sovereign (Planet)|Sovereign]]. She oversaw her fleet from the control center and strategized in there. *'''[[Birthing Chamber]]''': Ayesha used the birthing chamber to house a [[Birth Pods|Birth Pod]] for her creation, [[Adam Warlock|Adam]]. Following the [[Sovereign]]'s defeat at the hands of the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]], Ayesha retreated to the birthing chamber and was approached by her [[Sovereign Chambermaid|chambermaid]], deciding to reveal her creation. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Adam Warlock]] - Son/Creation ===Allies=== *[[Priestesses of the Sovereign]] - Subordinates **[[Sovereign Chambermaid]] *[[Sovereign Admiral]] - Subordinate *[[Zylak]] - Subordinate *[[Ravagers]] **[[Yondu Ravager Clan]] - Former Employees ***[[Taserface]] † *[[OrgoCorp]] **[[High Evolutionary]] - Creator and Killer **[[Vim]] † **[[Theel]] † **[[Karja]] **[[Kwol]] *[[War Pig]] † *[[Behemoth]] † {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Abilisks|Abilisk]] † *[[Guardians of the Galaxy]] - Former Employees and Attempted Victims **[[Star-Lord|Peter Quill/Star-Lord]] **[[Gamora]] † **[[Drax the Destroyer]] **[[Rocket]] **[[Groot]] **[[Mantis]] **[[Yondu Udonta]] † **[[Nebula]] - Former Prisoner *[[Kraglin Obfonteri]] - Former Employee *[[Ego]] † *[[Ravagers]] **[[Fitz-Gibbonok]] † **[[Gamora/Cosmic Time Heist|Gamora]] *[[Blurp]] {{Col-end}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Ayesha was created by the [[Enclave]], with the unacknowledged aid of [[Stephen Strange]], in order to resurrect their plan to end war and crime through the creation of an artificial human after the failure of the first attempt with "[[Adam Warlock|Him]]". Originally referred to as '''Paragon''', she would later be called other names like '''Her''', Ayesha and '''Kismet'''. **In the alternate future of [[w:c:marvel:Earth-691|Earth-691]], Ayesha is the biological mother of [[Stakar Ogord]]. ==References== {{Gallery|Ayesha/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP|Kismet (Marvel Comics)|Ayesha}} [[es:Ayesha]] [[fr:Ayesha]] [[ru:Аиша]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 Characters]] [[Category:Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Sovereign]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Golden Hair]] [[Category:Yellow Eyes]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]] [[Category:Characters Killed by High Evolutionary]]
